page: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a general-purpose server powered by the AMD EPYC 7542 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, operating under a KVM hypervisor. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing 6.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a 2048 GB SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-threaded performance. The server exhibits poor single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the bottom 10%. However, its multi-threaded capabilities are robust, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression reaching top-tier status in the top 10% at 461.92 GB/sec. Memory performance is strong, particularly in small-block cached reads at 3329.62 GB/sec and large-block writes at 88.04 GB/sec. Passmark testing shows strong results across extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model offers a high density of resources, though it introduces potential performance variability compared to dedicated cores. This server is well-suited for highly parallelized workloads that can leverage its 64 cores, such as multi-threaded decompression, database operations, Redis caching, and general-purpose web serving. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or intensive LLM text generation, where it demonstrates lower performance.
description: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a general-purpose server utilizing the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. Running on a KVM hypervisor, it delivers 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores at 2.0 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 2048 GB local SSD. While single-core performance is limited, the server excels in multi-threaded operations, achieving top-tier results in bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and strong memory write bandwidth. The shared CPU allocation provides a cost-effective path to high-density multi-core resources, though it presents a tradeoff in single-core execution speed. This configuration is highly effective for parallelized workloads, database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a general-purpose server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 2048 GB local SSD, delivering strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 2048 GB local SSD, optimized for highly parallelized workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core virtual server featuring balanced memory and local SSD storage for parallelized data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 384 GB of system memory
Top-tier multi-threaded decompression and strong memory write bandwidth
Shared CPU allocation under KVM virtualization
Optimized for parallel database operations and Redis caching
